revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}]], [[Nagano City|{{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|{{ill|Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|ja|松代町 (長野県)}}]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|{{ill|Lava dome|ja|溶岩円頂丘}}]] | First Ascent = }}
'''{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山}}''' (みなかみやま) is a '''volcanic mountain''' located in '''{{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan'''. It has an elevation of '''659 meters''', with a '''relative height of approximately 280 meters''' from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as '''"水上山" (Minakami-yama)'''.
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a '''lava dome''' composed of '''andesite''', which was active around '''300,000 to 350,000 years ago'''<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the '''high viscosity''' of its magma, the volcano did not produce '''ash eruptions or lava flows'''.
Between '''1965 and 1971''', the '''Matsushiro earthquake swarm''' occurred '''directly beneath Mount Minakami''', causing the mountain to '''uplift by about 1 meter'''. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by '''the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement'''. A '''gravity survey conducted in 1967''' found a '''low-gravity anomaly''' around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an '''elliptical subsidence structure''' measuring '''800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep''' is believed to exist underground.
'''Borehole drilling surveys''' have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s '''lava layer is about 150 meters thick''', and '''lake sediment deposits''' have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. '''Riverbed gravels''' have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a '''major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site''', serving as the '''headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition'''<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * '''Mid-slope:''' {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)}} * '''Summit:''' {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社}}, whose main shrine is '''{{ill|Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社}}''' * '''Base:''' {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社}}
During the '''final stages of World War II''', as '''Japan’s military situation worsened''', the government planned to '''relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family''' to '''Matsushiro'''. As a result, '''numerous underground bunkers''' were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its '''distinctive shape''', which differs from surrounding mountains, '''some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure'''. This has led to the theory that it is '''"the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times"'''. Every year on '''May 5th''', the local community hosts a '''"Pyramid Festival"''' celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to '''one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds''' for the '''{{ill|Japanese black salamander|ja|クロサンショウウオ}}'''<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * '''{{ill|Bio-Booster Armor Guyver|ja|強殖装甲ガイバー}}''' – The mountain appears under the name '''"Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)"''', located in the fictional '''"Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture"'''. It is depicted as the '''ruins of an alien race (the Uranus)''', who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * '''{{ill|Occult Academy|ja|世紀末オカルト学院}}''' – The anime series sets the fictional '''"Occult Academy"''' at the summit of Mount Minakami.
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} == See Also == {{Commonscat|Mount Minakami}} * {{ill|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters Site|ja|松代大本営跡}} * {{ill|Matsushiro Earthquake Swarm|ja|松代群発地震}} * {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency Matsushiro Earthquake Observatory|ja|気象庁松代地震観測所}} - {{ill|Matsushiro Earthquake Center|ja|松代地震センター}} * {{ill|Tsumaoyama|ja|妻女山}}
== External Links == * [http://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Quat_Vol/volcano_data/E40.html "Quaternary Volcano Data – Mount Minakami"] - Geological Survey of Japan, AIST * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ Minakami Shrine] (Official Website)

{{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}]], [[Nagano City|{{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|12=draft}}]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|{{ill|Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|ja|松代町 (長野県)|12=draft}}]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|{{ill|Lava dome|ja|溶岩円頂丘|12=draft}}]] | First Ascent = }}
'''{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=draft}}''' (みなかみやま) is a '''volcanic mountain''' located in '''{{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan'''. It has an elevation of '''659 meters''', with a '''relative height of approximately 280 meters''' from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as '''"水上山" (Minakami-yama)'''.
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a '''lava dome''' composed of '''andesite''', which was active around '''300,000 to 350,000 years ago'''<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the '''high viscosity''' of its magma, the volcano did not produce '''ash eruptions or lava flows'''.
Between '''1965 and 1971''', the '''Matsushiro earthquake swarm''' occurred '''directly beneath Mount Minakami''', causing the mountain to '''uplift by about 1 meter'''. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by '''the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement'''. A '''gravity survey conducted in 1967''' found a '''low-gravity anomaly''' around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an '''elliptical subsidence structure''' measuring '''800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep''' is believed to exist underground.
'''Borehole drilling surveys''' have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s '''lava layer is about 150 meters thick''', and '''lake sediment deposits''' have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. '''Riverbed gravels''' have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a '''major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site''', serving as the '''headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition'''<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * '''Mid-slope:''' {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=draft}} * '''Summit:''' {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|12=draft}}, whose main shrine is '''{{ill|Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=draft}}''' * '''Base:''' {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|12=draft}}
During the '''final stages of World War II''', as '''Japan’s military situation worsened''', the government planned to '''relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family''' to '''Matsushiro'''. As a result, '''numerous underground bunkers''' were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its '''distinctive shape''', which differs from surrounding mountains, '''some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure'''. This has led to the theory that it is '''"the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times"'''. Every year on '''May 5th''', the local community hosts a '''"Pyramid Festival"''' celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to '''one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds''' for the '''{{ill|Japanese black salamander|ja|クロサンショウウオ|12=draft}}'''<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=draft}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=draft}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * '''{{ill|Bio-Booster Armor Guyver|ja|強殖装甲ガイバー|12=draft}}''' – The mountain appears under the name '''"Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)"''', located in the fictional '''"Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture"'''. It is depicted as the '''ruins of an alien race (the Uranus)''', who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * '''{{ill|Occult Academy|ja|世紀末オカルト学院|12=draft}}''' – The anime series sets the fictional '''"Occult Academy"''' at the summit of Mount Minakami.

{{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}]], [[Nagano City|{{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|12=draft}}]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|{{ill|Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|ja|松代町 (長野県)|12=draft}}]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|{{ill|Lava dome|ja|溶岩円頂丘|12=draft}}]] | First Ascent = }}
'''{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=draft}}''' (みなかみやま) is a '''volcanic mountain''' located in '''{{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan'''. It has an elevation of '''659 meters''', with a '''relative height of approximately 280 meters''' from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as '''"水上山" (Minakami-yama)'''.
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a '''lava dome''' composed of '''andesite''', which was active around '''300,000 to 350,000 years ago'''<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the '''high viscosity''' of its magma, the volcano did not produce '''ash eruptions or lava flows'''.
Between '''1965 and 1971''', the '''Matsushiro earthquake swarm''' occurred '''directly beneath Mount Minakami''', causing the mountain to '''uplift by about 1 meter'''. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by '''the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement'''. A '''gravity survey conducted in 1967''' found a '''low-gravity anomaly''' around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an '''elliptical subsidence structure''' measuring '''800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep''' is believed to exist underground.
'''Borehole drilling surveys''' have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s '''lava layer is about 150 meters thick''', and '''lake sediment deposits''' have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. '''Riverbed gravels''' have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a '''major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site''', serving as the '''headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition'''<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * '''Mid-slope:''' {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=draft}} * '''Summit:''' {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|12=draft}}, whose main shrine is '''{{ill|Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=draft}}''' * '''Base:''' {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|12=draft}}
During the '''final stages of World War II''', as '''Japan’s military situation worsened''', the government planned to '''relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family''' to '''Matsushiro'''. As a result, '''numerous underground bunkers''' were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its '''distinctive shape''', which differs from surrounding mountains, '''some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure'''. This has led to the theory that it is '''"the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times"'''. Every year on '''May 5th''', the local community hosts a '''"Pyramid Festival"''' celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to '''one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds''' for the '''{{ill|Japanese black salamander|ja|クロサンショウウオ|12=draft}}'''<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=draft}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=draft}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * '''{{ill|Bio-Booster Armor Guyver|ja|強殖装甲ガイバー|12=draft}}''' – The mountain appears under the name '''"Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)"''', located in the fictional '''"Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture"'''. It is depicted as the '''ruins of an alien race (the Uranus)''', who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * '''{{ill|Occult Academy|ja|世紀末オカルト学院|12=draft}}''' – The anime series sets the fictional '''"Occult Academy"''' at the summit of Mount Minakami.

revision.text:
{{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}]], [[Nagano City|{{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|12=draft}}]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|{{ill|Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|ja|松代町 (長野県)|12=draft}}]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|{{ill|Lava dome|ja|溶岩円頂丘|12=draft}}]] | First Ascent = }}
'''{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=draft}}''' (みなかみやま) is a '''volcanic mountain''' located in '''{{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan'''. It has an elevation of '''659 meters''', with a '''relative height of approximately 280 meters''' from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as '''"水上山" (Minakami-yama)'''.
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a '''lava dome''' composed of '''andesite''', which was active around '''300,000 to 350,000 years ago'''<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the '''high viscosity''' of its magma, the volcano did not produce '''ash eruptions or lava flows'''.
Between '''1965 and 1971''', the '''Matsushiro earthquake swarm''' occurred '''directly beneath Mount Minakami''', causing the mountain to '''uplift by about 1 meter'''. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by '''the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement'''. A '''gravity survey conducted in 1967''' found a '''low-gravity anomaly''' around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an '''elliptical subsidence structure''' measuring '''800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep''' is believed to exist underground.
'''Borehole drilling surveys''' have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s '''lava layer is about 150 meters thick''', and '''lake sediment deposits''' have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. '''Riverbed gravels''' have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a '''major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site''', serving as the '''headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition'''<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * '''Mid-slope:''' {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=draft}} * '''Summit:''' {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|12=draft}}, whose main shrine is '''{{ill|Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=draft}}''' * '''Base:''' {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|12=draft}}
During the '''final stages of World War II''', as '''Japan’s military situation worsened''', the government planned to '''relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family''' to '''Matsushiro'''. As a result, '''numerous underground bunkers''' were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its '''distinctive shape''', which differs from surrounding mountains, '''some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure'''. This has led to the theory that it is '''"the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times"'''. Every year on '''May 5th''', the local community hosts a '''"Pyramid Festival"''' celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to '''one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds''' for the '''{{ill|Japanese black salamander|ja|クロサンショウウオ|12=draft}}'''<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=draft}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=draft}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * '''{{ill|Bio-Booster Armor Guyver|ja|強殖装甲ガイバー|12=draft}}''' – The mountain appears under the name '''"Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)"''', located in the fictional '''"Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture"'''. It is depicted as the '''ruins of an alien race (the Uranus)''', who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * '''{{ill|Occult Academy|ja|世紀末オカルト学院|12=draft}}''' – The anime series sets the fictional '''"Occult Academy"''' at the summit of Mount Minakami.

<!--Mount Minakami--> [[lld:Mont Minakami]]
{{draft categories|[[Category:Mountain lists]] [[Category:Mountains in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Nagano City]] [[Category:Lava domes]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] |12=draft}} [[ja:皆神山]] [[en:Mount Minakami]] [[sn:Mount Minakami]] [[de:Minakami (Berg)]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E7%9A%86%E7%A5%9E%E5%B1%B1%27%27%27]] [[sn:ount Minakami]]

{{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県|12=draft}}]], [[Nagano City|{{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|12=draft}}]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|{{ill|Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|ja|松代町 (長野県)|12=draft}}]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|{{ill|Lava dome|ja|溶岩円頂丘|12=draft}}]] | First Ascent = }}
'''{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=draft}}''' (みなかみやま) is a '''volcanic mountain''' located in '''{{ill|Nagano City|ja|長野市|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan'''. It has an elevation of '''659 meters''', with a '''relative height of approximately 280 meters''' from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as '''"水上山" (Minakami-yama)'''.
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a '''lava dome''' composed of '''andesite''', which was active around '''300,000 to 350,000 years ago'''<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the '''high viscosity''' of its magma, the volcano did not produce '''ash eruptions or lava flows'''.
Between '''1965 and 1971''', the '''Matsushiro earthquake swarm''' occurred '''directly beneath Mount Minakami''', causing the mountain to '''uplift by about 1 meter'''. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by '''the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement'''. A '''gravity survey conducted in 1967''' found a '''low-gravity anomaly''' around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an '''elliptical subsidence structure''' measuring '''800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep''' is believed to exist underground.
'''Borehole drilling surveys''' have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s '''lava layer is about 150 meters thick''', and '''lake sediment deposits''' have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. '''Riverbed gravels''' have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a '''major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site''', serving as the '''headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition'''<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * '''Mid-slope:''' {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=draft}} * '''Summit:''' {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|12=draft}}, whose main shrine is '''{{ill|Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=draft}}''' * '''Base:''' {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|12=draft}}
During the '''final stages of World War II''', as '''Japan’s military situation worsened''', the government planned to '''relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family''' to '''Matsushiro'''. As a result, '''numerous underground bunkers''' were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its '''distinctive shape''', which differs from surrounding mountains, '''some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure'''. This has led to the theory that it is '''"the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times"'''. Every year on '''May 5th''', the local community hosts a '''"Pyramid Festival"''' celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to '''one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds''' for the '''{{ill|Japanese black salamander|ja|クロサンショウウオ|12=draft}}'''<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=draft}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=draft}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * '''{{ill|Bio-Booster Armor Guyver|ja|強殖装甲ガイバー|12=draft}}''' – The mountain appears under the name '''"Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)"''', located in the fictional '''"Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture"'''. It is depicted as the '''ruins of an alien race (the Uranus)''', who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * '''{{ill|Occult Academy|ja|世紀末オカルト学院|12=draft}}''' – The anime series sets the fictional '''"Occult Academy"''' at the summit of Mount Minakami.

revision.text:
{{Infobox 山 |名称=皆神山 |画像=[[Image:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] |画像キャプション =北西から望む |標高=659 |地図={{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} |座標={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} |所在地=[[長野県]][[長野市]]・[[松代町 (長野県)|松代町]] |山系=独立峰 |種類=[[溶岩円頂丘|溶岩ドーム]] |初登頂= }}
'''皆神山'''（みなかみやま）は、[[長野県]][[長野市]][[松代町 (長野県)|松代]]、[[標高]]659メートルの[[火山]]である。麓の平地からの[[高さ#物理的な高さ|比高]]は280メートルほど。水上山と記す文献もある。
== 地理 == 30万年前から35万年前<ref group="注釈">カリウム・アルゴン法による年代測定</ref>あたりに活動した[[安山岩]]の[[溶岩ドーム]]である。安山岩は粘性の高い[[マグマ]]であったため、火山灰の噴出や溶岩流の流下は起こらなかった。
[[1965年]]から[[1971年]]にかけての[[松代群発地震]]が皆神山の直下で起り、この地震活動に伴い約1m隆起している。この地震は[[地下水|地下水脈]]の影響による[[岩盤]]の破壊と見られる。松代群発地震に伴い1967年に付近の重力分布調査が行われ、皆神山付近には低重力域があり<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf 松代群発地震地域における重力調査概報 究速報第 5 け, 1967 ]}}防災科学技術研究所 </ref>地下には、短径800m、長径1500m、深度200ないし400mの楕円形陥没構造の存在が推定される。[[ボーリング|ボーリング調査]]により、皆神山溶岩は150m程度の厚さがあることが確認されており、その下に湖水堆積物が見つかっている<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm おまんじゅうのような山 --皆神山]長野の大地みどころ100選</ref>。また、山頂部には河床礫が見られる<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html 松代 皆神山]長野県理化学会 地学部会 編 長野県の地学</ref>。
近代以前には[[修験道]]の一大拠点であり、皆神修験の本拠地になっていた<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=博物館だより第125号|publisher=長野市立博物館 |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>。中腹には[[岩戸神社 (長野市)|岩戸神社]]、山頂には熊野出速雄神社を本社とする[[皆神神社]]がある。また麓には[[藤沢萩神社]]が鎮座する。
[[第二次世界大戦]]末期には[[日本]]の戦況が悪くなり松代が[[大本営]]・[[政府]]・[[皇室]]を含む[[首都]]移転の予定地となり、皆神山と周辺の山には多数の[[地下壕]]が掘られた。 {{see also|松代大本営}}
周囲の山並みと異なる溶岩ドームの山容から、人工物と思い込む者が現われ、「太古に作られた世界最大の[[ピラミッド]]」という説が起こる。毎年[[5月5日]]には、地元自治会により『ピラミッド祭り』が開催される。
また全国的にも珍しい標高の低い土地での[[クロサンショウウオ]]の産卵池がある<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html 皆神山のクロサンショウウオ産卵池]長野市文化財データベース 2019年9月17日閲覧</ref>。
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|長野盆地と皆神山(右下)の地形図 File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|岩戸神社、洞穴はピラミッドの入り口とも一部でいわれている File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|熊野出速雄神社（皆神神社）の鳥居 </gallery>
== フィクション == * [[強殖装甲ガイバー]] - N県N市'''竹代町'''という架空の地名の土地に「'''魅奈神山'''」として登場。地球生物の兵器化を企て、研究実験の末に人類を作り出した異星人「降臨者（ウラノス）」の遺跡が存在する。 * [[世紀末オカルト学院]] - 同山の山頂に学園があるという設定。
==脚注== {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Mount Minakami}} * [[松代大本営跡]] * [[松代群発地震]] * [[気象庁松代地震観測所]] - [[松代地震センター]] * [[妻女山]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://gbank.gsj.jp/volcano/Quat_Vol/volcano_data/E40.html 第四紀火山「皆神山」] - 産業技術総合研究所 地質総合センター * [http://www.minakami-jinja.jp/ 皆神神社]（公式サイト）
{{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:みなかみやま}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:長野県の山]] [[Category:長野市の地理]] [[Category:溶岩ドーム]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[皆神山]] to [[Mount Minakami]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q728107]] Wikidata name: Mount Minakami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]]], [[Nagano City|[[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]]]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|[[:en:Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro Town (Nagano)]]]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|[[:en:Lava dome|Lava dome]]]] | First Ascent = }}
'''{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}}''' (みなかみやま) is a '''volcanic mountain''' located in '''[[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture, Japan'''. It has an elevation of '''659 meters''', with a '''relative height of approximately 280 meters''' from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as '''"水上山" (Minakami-yama)'''.
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a '''lava dome''' composed of '''andesite''', which was active around '''300,000 to 350,000 years ago'''<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the '''high viscosity''' of its magma, the volcano did not produce '''ash eruptions or lava flows'''.
Between '''1965 and 1971''', the '''Matsushiro earthquake swarm''' occurred '''directly beneath Mount Minakami''', causing the mountain to '''uplift by about 1 meter'''. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by '''the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement'''. A '''gravity survey conducted in 1967''' found a '''low-gravity anomaly''' around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an '''elliptical subsidence structure''' measuring '''800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep''' is believed to exist underground.
'''Borehole drilling surveys''' have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s '''lava layer is about 150 meters thick''', and '''lake sediment deposits''' have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. '''Riverbed gravels''' have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a '''major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site''', serving as the '''headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition'''<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * '''Mid-slope:''' {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * '''Summit:''' {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is '''{{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}}''' * '''Base:''' {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the '''final stages of World War II''', as '''Japan’s military situation worsened''', the government planned to '''relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family''' to '''Matsushiro'''. As a result, '''numerous underground bunkers''' were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its '''distinctive shape''', which differs from surrounding mountains, '''some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure'''. This has led to the theory that it is '''"the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times"'''. Every year on '''May 5th''', the local community hosts a '''"Pyramid Festival"''' celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to '''one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds''' for the '''[[:en:Japanese black salamander|Japanese black salamander]]'''<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * '''[[:en:Bio Booster Armor Guyver|Bio-Booster Armor Guyver]]''' – The mountain appears under the name '''"Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)"''', located in the fictional '''"Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture"'''. It is depicted as the '''ruins of an alien race (the Uranus)''', who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * '''[[:en:Occult Academy|Occult Academy]]''' – The anime series sets the fictional '''"Occult Academy"''' at the summit of Mount Minakami.
== References ==
=== Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}}
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Mount Minakami}} * [[:en:Matsushiro Underground Imperial Headquarters|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters Site]] * [[:en:Matsushiro earthquake swarm|Matsushiro Earthquake Swarm]] * {{ill|Japan Meteorological Agency Matsushiro Earthquake Observatory|ja|気象庁松代地震観測所|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Meteorological Agency Matsushiro Earthquake Observatory|qq=|1=Matsushiro Seismological Observatory|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11548110}} - {{ill|Matsushiro Earthquake Center|ja|松代地震センター|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsushiro Earthquake Center|qq=|1=Matsushiro Earthquake Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529233}} * {{ill|Tsumaoyama|ja|妻女山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsumaoyama|qq=|1=Tsumaonna Mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11447188}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q728107]] Wikidata name: Mount Minakami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]]], [[Nagano City|[[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]]]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|[[:en:Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro Town (Nagano)]]]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|[[:en:Lava dome|Lava dome]]]] | First Ascent = }}
'''{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}}''' (みなかみやま) is a '''volcanic mountain''' located in '''[[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture, Japan'''. It has an elevation of '''659 meters''', with a '''relative height of approximately 280 meters''' from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as '''"水上山" (Minakami-yama)'''.
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a '''lava dome''' composed of '''andesite''', which was active around '''300,000 to 350,000 years ago'''<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the '''high viscosity''' of its magma, the volcano did not produce '''ash eruptions or lava flows'''.
Between '''1965 and 1971''', the '''Matsushiro earthquake swarm''' occurred '''directly beneath Mount Minakami''', causing the mountain to '''uplift by about 1 meter'''. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by '''the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement'''. A '''gravity survey conducted in 1967''' found a '''low-gravity anomaly''' around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an '''elliptical subsidence structure''' measuring '''800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep''' is believed to exist underground.
'''Borehole drilling surveys''' have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s '''lava layer is about 150 meters thick''', and '''lake sediment deposits''' have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. '''Riverbed gravels''' have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a '''major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site''', serving as the '''headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition'''<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * '''Mid-slope:''' {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * '''Summit:''' {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is '''{{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}}''' * '''Base:''' {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the '''final stages of World War II''', as '''Japan’s military situation worsened''', the government planned to '''relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family''' to '''Matsushiro'''. As a result, '''numerous underground bunkers''' were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its '''distinctive shape''', which differs from surrounding mountains, '''some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure'''. This has led to the theory that it is '''"the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times"'''. Every year on '''May 5th''', the local community hosts a '''"Pyramid Festival"''' celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to '''one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds''' for the '''[[:en:Japanese black salamander|Japanese black salamander]]'''<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * '''[[:en:Bio Booster Armor Guyver|Bio-Booster Armor Guyver]]''' – The mountain appears under the name '''"Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)"''', located in the fictional '''"Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture"'''. It is depicted as the '''ruins of an alien race (the Uranus)''', who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * '''[[:en:Occult Academy|Occult Academy]]''' – The anime series sets the fictional '''"Occult Academy"''' at the summit of Mount Minakami.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q728107]] Wikidata name: Mount Minakami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]]], [[Nagano City|[[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]]]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|[[:en:Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro Town (Nagano)]]]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|[[:en:Lava dome|Lava dome]]]] | First Ascent = }}
{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami}} (みなかみやま) is a volcanic mountain located in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture, Japan. It has an elevation of 659 meters, with a relative height of approximately 280 meters from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as "水上山" (Minakami-yama).
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a lava dome composed of andesite, which was active around 300,000 to 350,000 years ago<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the high viscosity of its magma, the volcano did not produce ash eruptions or lava flows.
Between 1965 and 1971, the Matsushiro earthquake swarm occurred directly beneath Mount Minakami, causing the mountain to uplift by about 1 meter. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement. A gravity survey conducted in 1967 found a low-gravity anomaly around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an elliptical subsidence structure measuring 800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep is believed to exist underground.
Borehole drilling surveys have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s lava layer is about 150 meters thick, and lake sediment deposits have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. Riverbed gravels have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site, serving as the headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * Mid-slope: {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * Summit: {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} * Base: {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the final stages of World War II, as Japan’s military situation worsened, the government planned to relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family to Matsushiro. As a result, numerous underground bunkers were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its distinctive shape, which differs from surrounding mountains, some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure. This has led to the theory that it is "the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times". Every year on May 5th, the local community hosts a "Pyramid Festival" celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds for the [[:en:Japanese black salamander|Japanese black salamander]]<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * [[:en:Bio Booster Armor Guyver|Bio-Booster Armor Guyver]] – The mountain appears under the name "Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)", located in the fictional "Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture". It is depicted as the ruins of an alien race (the Uranus), who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * [[:en:Occult Academy|Occult Academy]] – The anime series sets the fictional "Occult Academy" at the summit of Mount Minakami.

{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami}} (みなかみやま) is a volcanic mountain located in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture, Japan. It has an elevation of 659 meters, with a relative height of approximately 280 meters from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as "水上山" (Minakami-yama).
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a lava dome composed of andesite, which was active around 300,000 to 350,000 years ago<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the high viscosity of its magma, the volcano did not produce ash eruptions or lava flows.
Between 1965 and 1971, the Matsushiro earthquake swarm occurred directly beneath Mount Minakami, causing the mountain to uplift by about 1 meter. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement. A gravity survey conducted in 1967 found a low-gravity anomaly around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an elliptical subsidence structure measuring 800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep is believed to exist underground.
Borehole drilling surveys have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s lava layer is about 150 meters thick, and lake sediment deposits have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. Riverbed gravels have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site, serving as the headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * Mid-slope: {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * Summit: {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} * Base: {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the final stages of World War II, as Japan’s military situation worsened, the government planned to relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family to Matsushiro. As a result, numerous underground bunkers were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its distinctive shape, which differs from surrounding mountains, some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure. This has led to the theory that it is "the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times". Every year on May 5th, the local community hosts a "Pyramid Festival" celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds for the [[:en:Japanese black salamander|Japanese black salamander]]<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * [[:en:Bio Booster Armor Guyver|Bio-Booster Armor Guyver]] – The mountain appears under the name "Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)", located in the fictional "Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture". It is depicted as the ruins of an alien race (the Uranus), who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * [[:en:Occult Academy|Occult Academy]] – The anime series sets the fictional "Occult Academy" at the summit of Mount Minakami.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q728107]] Wikidata name: Mount Minakami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]]], [[Nagano City|[[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]]]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|[[:en:Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro Town (Nagano)]]]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|[[:en:Lava dome|Lava dome]]]] | First Ascent = }}
{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami}} (みなかみやま) is a volcanic mountain located in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture, Japan. It has an elevation of 659 meters, with a relative height of approximately 280 meters from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as "水上山" (Minakami-yama).
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a lava dome composed of andesite, which was active around 300,000 to 350,000 years ago<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the high viscosity of its magma, the volcano did not produce ash eruptions or lava flows.
Between 1965 and 1971, the Matsushiro earthquake swarm occurred directly beneath Mount Minakami, causing the mountain to uplift by about 1 meter. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement. A gravity survey conducted in 1967 found a low-gravity anomaly around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an elliptical subsidence structure measuring 800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep is believed to exist underground.
Borehole drilling surveys have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s lava layer is about 150 meters thick, and lake sediment deposits have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. Riverbed gravels have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site, serving as the headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * Mid-slope: {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * Summit: {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} * Base: {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the final stages of World War II, as Japan’s military situation worsened, the government planned to relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family to Matsushiro. As a result, numerous underground bunkers were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its distinctive shape, which differs from surrounding mountains, some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure. This has led to the theory that it is "the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times". Every year on May 5th, the local community hosts a "Pyramid Festival" celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds for the [[:en:Japanese black salamander|Japanese black salamander]]<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * [[:en:Bio Booster Armor Guyver|Bio-Booster Armor Guyver]] – The mountain appears under the name "Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)", located in the fictional "Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture". It is depicted as the ruins of an alien race (the Uranus), who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * [[:en:Occult Academy|Occult Academy]] – The anime series sets the fictional "Occult Academy" at the summit of Mount Minakami.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q728107]] Wikidata name: Mount Minakami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]]], [[Nagano City|[[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]]]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|[[:en:Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro Town (Nagano)]]]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|[[:en:Lava dome|Lava dome]]]] | First Ascent = }}
{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami}} (みなかみやま) is a volcanic mountain located in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture, Japan. It has an elevation of 659 meters, with a relative height of approximately 280 meters from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as "水上山" (Minakami-yama).
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a lava dome composed of andesite, which was active around 300,000 to 350,000 years ago<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the high viscosity of its magma, the volcano did not produce ash eruptions or lava flows.
Between 1965 and 1971, the Matsushiro earthquake swarm occurred directly beneath Mount Minakami, causing the mountain to uplift by about 1 meter. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement. A gravity survey conducted in 1967 found a low-gravity anomaly around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an elliptical subsidence structure measuring 800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep is believed to exist underground.
Borehole drilling surveys have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s lava layer is about 150 meters thick, and lake sediment deposits have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. Riverbed gravels have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site, serving as the headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * Mid-slope: {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * Summit: {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} * Base: {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the final stages of World War II, as Japan’s military situation worsened, the government planned to relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family to Matsushiro. As a result, numerous underground bunkers were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its distinctive shape, which differs from surrounding mountains, some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure. This has led to the theory that it is "the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times". Every year on May 5th, the local community hosts a "Pyramid Festival" celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds for the [[:en:Japanese black salamander|Japanese black salamander]]<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * [[:en:Bio Booster Armor Guyver|Bio-Booster Armor Guyver]] – The mountain appears under the name "Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)", located in the fictional "Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture". It is depicted as the ruins of an alien race (the Uranus), who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * [[:en:Occult Academy|Occult Academy]] – The anime series sets the fictional "Occult Academy" at the summit of Mount Minakami.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q728107]] Wikidata name: Mount Minakami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]]], [[Nagano City|[[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]]]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|[[:en:Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro Town (Nagano)]]]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|[[:en:Lava dome|Lava dome]]]] | First Ascent = }}
{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami}} (みなかみやま) is a volcanic mountain located in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture, Japan. It has an elevation of 659 meters, with a relative height of approximately 280 meters from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as "水上山" (Minakami-yama).
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a lava dome composed of andesite, which was active around 300,000 to 350,000 years ago<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the high viscosity of its magma, the volcano did not produce ash eruptions or lava flows.
Between 1965 and 1971, the Matsushiro earthquake swarm occurred directly beneath Mount Minakami, causing the mountain to uplift by about 1 meter. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement. A gravity survey conducted in 1967 found a low-gravity anomaly around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an elliptical subsidence structure measuring 800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep is believed to exist underground.
Borehole drilling surveys have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s lava layer is about 150 meters thick, and lake sediment deposits have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. Riverbed gravels have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site, serving as the headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * Mid-slope: {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * Summit: {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} * Base: {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the final stages of World War II, as Japan’s military situation worsened, the government planned to relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family to Matsushiro. As a result, numerous underground bunkers were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its distinctive shape, which differs from surrounding mountains, some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure. This has led to the theory that it is "the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times". Every year on May 5th, the local community hosts a "Pyramid Festival" celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds for the [[:en:Japanese black salamander|Japanese black salamander]]<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * [[:en:Bio Booster Armor Guyver|Bio-Booster Armor Guyver]] – The mountain appears under the name "Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)", located in the fictional "Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture". It is depicted as the ruins of an alien race (the Uranus), who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * [[:en:Occult Academy|Occult Academy]] – The anime series sets the fictional "Occult Academy" at the summit of Mount Minakami.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q728107]] Wikidata name: Mount Minakami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|[[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]]], [[Nagano City|[[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]]]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|[[:en:Matsushiro, Nagano|Matsushiro Town (Nagano)]]]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|[[:en:Lava dome|Lava dome]]]] | First Ascent = }}
{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami}} (みなかみやま) is a volcanic mountain located in [[:en:Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], Nagano Prefecture, Japan. It has an elevation of 659 meters, with a relative height of approximately 280 meters from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as "水上山" (Minakami-yama).
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a lava dome composed of andesite, which was active around 300,000 to 350,000 years ago<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the high viscosity of its magma, the volcano did not produce ash eruptions or lava flows.
Between 1965 and 1971, the Matsushiro earthquake swarm occurred directly beneath Mount Minakami, causing the mountain to uplift by about 1 meter. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement. A gravity survey conducted in 1967 found a low-gravity anomaly around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an elliptical subsidence structure measuring 800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep is believed to exist underground.
Borehole drilling surveys have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s lava layer is about 150 meters thick, and lake sediment deposits have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. Riverbed gravels have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site, serving as the headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * Mid-slope: {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * Summit: {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} * Base: {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the final stages of World War II, as Japan’s military situation worsened, the government planned to relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family to Matsushiro. As a result, numerous underground bunkers were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its distinctive shape, which differs from surrounding mountains, some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure. This has led to the theory that it is "the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times". Every year on May 5th, the local community hosts a "Pyramid Festival" celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds for the [[:en:Japanese black salamander|Japanese black salamander]]<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * [[:en:Bio Booster Armor Guyver|Bio-Booster Armor Guyver]] – The mountain appears under the name "Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)", located in the fictional "Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture". It is depicted as the ruins of an alien race (the Uranus), who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * [[:en:Occult Academy|Occult Academy]] – The anime series sets the fictional "Occult Academy" at the summit of Mount Minakami.
