revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]]
{{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}]], [[Nagano City|{{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City}}]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|{{ill|Matsushiro, Nagano|en|Matsushiro, Nagano|WD=Q11529238|lt=Matsushiro Town (Nagano)}}]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|{{ill|Lava dome|en|Lava dome|WD=Q534282|lt=Lava dome}}]] | First Ascent = }}
{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami}} (みなかみやま) is a volcanic mountain located in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. It has an elevation of 659 meters, with a relative height of approximately 280 meters from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as "水上山" (Minakami-yama).
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a lava dome composed of andesite, which was active around 300,000 to 350,000 years ago<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the high viscosity of its magma, the volcano did not produce ash eruptions or lava flows.
Between 1965 and 1971, the Matsushiro earthquake swarm occurred directly beneath Mount Minakami, causing the mountain to uplift by about 1 meter. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement. A gravity survey conducted in 1967 found a low-gravity anomaly around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an elliptical subsidence structure measuring 800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep is believed to exist underground.
Borehole drilling surveys have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s lava layer is about 150 meters thick, and lake sediment deposits have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. Riverbed gravels have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site, serving as the headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * Mid-slope: {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * Summit: {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} * Base: {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the final stages of World War II, as Japan’s military situation worsened, the government planned to relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family to Matsushiro. As a result, numerous underground bunkers were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its distinctive shape, which differs from surrounding mountains, some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure. This has led to the theory that it is "the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times". Every year on May 5th, the local community hosts a "Pyramid Festival" celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds for the {{ill|Japanese black salamander|en|Japanese black salamander|WD=Q2276273|lt=Japanese black salamander}}<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * {{ill|Bio Booster Armor Guyver|en|Bio Booster Armor Guyver|WD=Q2632121|lt=Bio-Booster Armor Guyver}} – The mountain appears under the name "Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)", located in the fictional "Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture". It is depicted as the ruins of an alien race (the Uranus), who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * {{ill|Occult Academy|en|Occult Academy|WD=Q1523630|lt=Occult Academy}} – The anime series sets the fictional "Occult Academy" at the summit of Mount Minakami.

{{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}]], [[Nagano City|{{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|{{ill|Matsushiro, Nagano|en|Matsushiro, Nagano|WD=Q11529238|lt=Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|1=Matsushiro, Nagano}}]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|{{ill|Lava dome|en|Lava dome|WD=Q534282|lt=Lava dome|1=Lava dome}}]] | First Ascent = }}
{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami}} (みなかみやま) is a volcanic mountain located in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. It has an elevation of 659 meters, with a relative height of approximately 280 meters from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as "水上山" (Minakami-yama).
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a lava dome composed of andesite, which was active around 300,000 to 350,000 years ago<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the high viscosity of its magma, the volcano did not produce ash eruptions or lava flows.
Between 1965 and 1971, the Matsushiro earthquake swarm occurred directly beneath Mount Minakami, causing the mountain to uplift by about 1 meter. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement. A gravity survey conducted in 1967 found a low-gravity anomaly around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an elliptical subsidence structure measuring 800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep is believed to exist underground.
Borehole drilling surveys have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s lava layer is about 150 meters thick, and lake sediment deposits have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. Riverbed gravels have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site, serving as the headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * Mid-slope: {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * Summit: {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} * Base: {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the final stages of World War II, as Japan’s military situation worsened, the government planned to relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family to Matsushiro. As a result, numerous underground bunkers were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its distinctive shape, which differs from surrounding mountains, some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure. This has led to the theory that it is "the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times". Every year on May 5th, the local community hosts a "Pyramid Festival" celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds for the {{ill|Japanese black salamander|en|Japanese black salamander|WD=Q2276273|lt=Japanese black salamander|1=Japanese black salamander}}<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * {{ill|Bio Booster Armor Guyver|en|Bio Booster Armor Guyver|WD=Q2632121|lt=Bio-Booster Armor Guyver|1=Bio Booster Armor Guyver}} – The mountain appears under the name "Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)", located in the fictional "Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture". It is depicted as the ruins of an alien race (the Uranus), who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * {{ill|Occult Academy|en|Occult Academy|WD=Q1523630|lt=Occult Academy|1=Occult Academy}} – The anime series sets the fictional "Occult Academy" at the summit of Mount Minakami.

{{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}]], [[Nagano City|{{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|{{ill|Matsushiro, Nagano|en|Matsushiro, Nagano|WD=Q11529238|lt=Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|1=Matsushiro, Nagano}}]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|{{ill|Lava dome|en|Lava dome|WD=Q534282|lt=Lava dome|1=Lava dome}}]] | First Ascent = }}
{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami}} (みなかみやま) is a volcanic mountain located in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. It has an elevation of 659 meters, with a relative height of approximately 280 meters from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as "水上山" (Minakami-yama).
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a lava dome composed of andesite, which was active around 300,000 to 350,000 years ago<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the high viscosity of its magma, the volcano did not produce ash eruptions or lava flows.
Between 1965 and 1971, the Matsushiro earthquake swarm occurred directly beneath Mount Minakami, causing the mountain to uplift by about 1 meter. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement. A gravity survey conducted in 1967 found a low-gravity anomaly around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an elliptical subsidence structure measuring 800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep is believed to exist underground.
Borehole drilling surveys have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s lava layer is about 150 meters thick, and lake sediment deposits have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. Riverbed gravels have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site, serving as the headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * Mid-slope: {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * Summit: {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} * Base: {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the final stages of World War II, as Japan’s military situation worsened, the government planned to relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family to Matsushiro. As a result, numerous underground bunkers were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its distinctive shape, which differs from surrounding mountains, some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure. This has led to the theory that it is "the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times". Every year on May 5th, the local community hosts a "Pyramid Festival" celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds for the {{ill|Japanese black salamander|en|Japanese black salamander|WD=Q2276273|lt=Japanese black salamander|1=Japanese black salamander}}<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * {{ill|Bio Booster Armor Guyver|en|Bio Booster Armor Guyver|WD=Q2632121|lt=Bio-Booster Armor Guyver|1=Bio Booster Armor Guyver}} – The mountain appears under the name "Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)", located in the fictional "Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture". It is depicted as the ruins of an alien race (the Uranus), who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * {{ill|Occult Academy|en|Occult Academy|WD=Q1523630|lt=Occult Academy|1=Occult Academy}} – The anime series sets the fictional "Occult Academy" at the summit of Mount Minakami.

{{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}]], [[Nagano City|{{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|{{ill|Matsushiro, Nagano|en|Matsushiro, Nagano|WD=Q11529238|lt=Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|1=Matsushiro, Nagano}}]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|{{ill|Lava dome|en|Lava dome|WD=Q534282|lt=Lava dome|1=Lava dome}}]] | First Ascent = }}
{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami}} (みなかみやま) is a volcanic mountain located in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. It has an elevation of 659 meters, with a relative height of approximately 280 meters from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as "水上山" (Minakami-yama).
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a lava dome composed of andesite, which was active around 300,000 to 350,000 years ago<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the high viscosity of its magma, the volcano did not produce ash eruptions or lava flows.
Between 1965 and 1971, the Matsushiro earthquake swarm occurred directly beneath Mount Minakami, causing the mountain to uplift by about 1 meter. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement. A gravity survey conducted in 1967 found a low-gravity anomaly around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an elliptical subsidence structure measuring 800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep is believed to exist underground.
Borehole drilling surveys have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s lava layer is about 150 meters thick, and lake sediment deposits have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. Riverbed gravels have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site, serving as the headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * Mid-slope: {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * Summit: {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} * Base: {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the final stages of World War II, as Japan’s military situation worsened, the government planned to relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family to Matsushiro. As a result, numerous underground bunkers were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its distinctive shape, which differs from surrounding mountains, some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure. This has led to the theory that it is "the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times". Every year on May 5th, the local community hosts a "Pyramid Festival" celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds for the {{ill|Japanese black salamander|en|Japanese black salamander|WD=Q2276273|lt=Japanese black salamander|1=Japanese black salamander}}<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * {{ill|Bio Booster Armor Guyver|en|Bio Booster Armor Guyver|WD=Q2632121|lt=Bio-Booster Armor Guyver|1=Bio Booster Armor Guyver}} – The mountain appears under the name "Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)", located in the fictional "Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture". It is depicted as the ruins of an alien race (the Uranus), who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * {{ill|Occult Academy|en|Occult Academy|WD=Q1523630|lt=Occult Academy|1=Occult Academy}} – The anime series sets the fictional "Occult Academy" at the summit of Mount Minakami.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]]
{{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}]], [[Nagano City|{{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|{{ill|Matsushiro, Nagano|en|Matsushiro, Nagano|WD=Q11529238|lt=Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|1=Matsushiro, Nagano}}]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|{{ill|Lava dome|en|Lava dome|WD=Q534282|lt=Lava dome|1=Lava dome}}]] | First Ascent = }}
{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami}} (みなかみやま) is a volcanic mountain located in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. It has an elevation of 659 meters, with a relative height of approximately 280 meters from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as "水上山" (Minakami-yama).
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a lava dome composed of andesite, which was active around 300,000 to 350,000 years ago<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the high viscosity of its magma, the volcano did not produce ash eruptions or lava flows.
Between 1965 and 1971, the Matsushiro earthquake swarm occurred directly beneath Mount Minakami, causing the mountain to uplift by about 1 meter. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement. A gravity survey conducted in 1967 found a low-gravity anomaly around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an elliptical subsidence structure measuring 800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep is believed to exist underground.
Borehole drilling surveys have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s lava layer is about 150 meters thick, and lake sediment deposits have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. Riverbed gravels have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site, serving as the headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * Mid-slope: {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * Summit: {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} * Base: {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the final stages of World War II, as Japan’s military situation worsened, the government planned to relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family to Matsushiro. As a result, numerous underground bunkers were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its distinctive shape, which differs from surrounding mountains, some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure. This has led to the theory that it is "the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times". Every year on May 5th, the local community hosts a "Pyramid Festival" celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds for the {{ill|Japanese black salamander|en|Japanese black salamander|WD=Q2276273|lt=Japanese black salamander|1=Japanese black salamander}}<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * {{ill|Bio Booster Armor Guyver|en|Bio Booster Armor Guyver|WD=Q2632121|lt=Bio-Booster Armor Guyver|1=Bio Booster Armor Guyver}} – The mountain appears under the name "Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)", located in the fictional "Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture". It is depicted as the ruins of an alien race (the Uranus), who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * {{ill|Occult Academy|en|Occult Academy|WD=Q1523630|lt=Occult Academy|1=Occult Academy}} – The anime series sets the fictional "Occult Academy" at the summit of Mount Minakami.

{{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}]], [[Nagano City|{{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|qid=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|{{ill|Matsushiro, Nagano|en|Matsushiro, Nagano|qid=Q11529238|lt=Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|1=Matsushiro, Nagano}}]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|{{ill|Lava dome|en|Lava dome|qid=Q534282|lt=Lava dome|1=Lava dome}}]] | First Ascent = }}
{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami}} (みなかみやま) is a volcanic mountain located in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|qid=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. It has an elevation of 659 meters, with a relative height of approximately 280 meters from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as "水上山" (Minakami-yama).
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a lava dome composed of andesite, which was active around 300,000 to 350,000 years ago<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the high viscosity of its magma, the volcano did not produce ash eruptions or lava flows.
Between 1965 and 1971, the Matsushiro earthquake swarm occurred directly beneath Mount Minakami, causing the mountain to uplift by about 1 meter. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement. A gravity survey conducted in 1967 found a low-gravity anomaly around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an elliptical subsidence structure measuring 800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep is believed to exist underground.
Borehole drilling surveys have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s lava layer is about 150 meters thick, and lake sediment deposits have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. Riverbed gravels have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site, serving as the headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * Mid-slope: {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * Summit: {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} * Base: {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the final stages of World War II, as Japan’s military situation worsened, the government planned to relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family to Matsushiro. As a result, numerous underground bunkers were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its distinctive shape, which differs from surrounding mountains, some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure. This has led to the theory that it is "the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times". Every year on May 5th, the local community hosts a "Pyramid Festival" celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds for the {{ill|Japanese black salamander|en|Japanese black salamander|qid=Q2276273|lt=Japanese black salamander|1=Japanese black salamander}}<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * {{ill|Bio Booster Armor Guyver|en|Bio Booster Armor Guyver|qid=Q2632121|lt=Bio-Booster Armor Guyver|1=Bio Booster Armor Guyver}} – The mountain appears under the name "Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)", located in the fictional "Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture". It is depicted as the ruins of an alien race (the Uranus), who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * {{ill|Occult Academy|en|Occult Academy|qid=Q1523630|lt=Occult Academy|1=Occult Academy}} – The anime series sets the fictional "Occult Academy" at the summit of Mount Minakami.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Mount Minakami]][[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Minakami (Berg)]][[ja:皆神山]][[lld:Mont Minakami]]
{{Infobox mountain | Name = {{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami|qq=}} | image = [[File:Minakamiyama_1.jpg|300px]] | Caption = View from the northwest | Elevation = 659m | Map = {{location map |Japan#Japan Nagano Prefecture |mark = RedMountain.svg |marksize =12 |lat_deg= 36|lat_min= 33|lat_sec= 14|lat_dir=N |lon_deg= 138|lon_min= 13|lon_sec= 21|lon_dir=E |width=300 |relief=1 }} | Coordinates = {{Coord|36|33|14|N|138|13|21|E|region:JP-20}} | Location = [[Nagano Prefecture|{{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}]], [[Nagano City|{{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|qid=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}]], [[Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|{{ill|Matsushiro, Nagano|en|Matsushiro, Nagano|qid=Q11529238|lt=Matsushiro Town (Nagano)|1=Matsushiro, Nagano}}]] | Mountain Type = [[Lava dome|{{ill|Lava dome|en|Lava dome|qid=Q534282|lt=Lava dome|1=Lava dome}}]] | First Ascent = }}
{{ill|Mount Minakami|ja|皆神山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Minakami}} (みなかみやま) is a volcanic mountain located in {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|qid=Q128849|lt=Nagano City|1=Nagano (city)}}, Nagano Prefecture, Japan. It has an elevation of 659 meters, with a relative height of approximately 280 meters from the surrounding plains. Some historical documents also refer to it as "水上山" (Minakami-yama).
== Geography == Mount Minakami is a lava dome composed of andesite, which was active around 300,000 to 350,000 years ago<ref group="Note">Based on potassium-argon dating.</ref>. Due to the high viscosity of its magma, the volcano did not produce ash eruptions or lava flows.
Between 1965 and 1971, the Matsushiro earthquake swarm occurred directly beneath Mount Minakami, causing the mountain to uplift by about 1 meter. This seismic activity is believed to have been caused by the destruction of bedrock due to underground water movement. A gravity survey conducted in 1967 found a low-gravity anomaly around Mount Minakami<ref>{{PDFlink|[https://dil-opac.bosai.go.jp/publication/nrcdp/sougou_sokuhou/05gou/05_02.pdf Matsushiro Earthquake Region Gravity Survey Report, 1967]}} - National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience</ref>. Based on this data, an elliptical subsidence structure measuring 800m in short diameter, 1500m in long diameter, and extending 200–400m deep is believed to exist underground.
Borehole drilling surveys have confirmed that Mount Minakami’s lava layer is about 150 meters thick, and lake sediment deposits have been discovered beneath it<ref>[http://www.janis.or.jp/users/gann/tisitu/100sen/oka_1/minakamiyama.htm "A Mountain Like a Steamed Bun – Mount Minakami"] - 100 Scenic Geological Sites of Nagano</ref>. Riverbed gravels have also been observed at the summit<ref>[http://www2.ueda.ne.jp/~moa/matusiro.html "Matsushiro - Mount Minakami"] - Nagano Prefecture Science Association</ref>.
Before the modern era, Mount Minakami was a major Shugendō (mountain asceticism) site, serving as the headquarters of the Minakami Shugendō tradition<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.city.nagano.nagano.jp/museum/pdf/dayori/125.pdf |title=Museum Bulletin No. 125 |publisher=Nagano City Museum |page=1 |accessdate=2023-11-11}}</ref>. Several shrines are located on the mountain: * Mid-slope: {{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}} * Summit: {{ill|Minakami Shrine|ja|皆神神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minakami Shrine|qq=}}, whose main shrine is {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} * Base: {{ill|Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|ja|藤沢萩神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujisawa Hagi Shrine|qq=}}
During the final stages of World War II, as Japan’s military situation worsened, the government planned to relocate the Imperial Headquarters, government, and Imperial Family to Matsushiro. As a result, numerous underground bunkers were dug into Mount Minakami and nearby mountains. {{see also|Matsushiro Imperial Headquarters}}
Due to its distinctive shape, which differs from surrounding mountains, some people mistakenly believe Mount Minakami to be an artificial structure. This has led to the theory that it is "the world's largest pyramid built in ancient times". Every year on May 5th, the local community hosts a "Pyramid Festival" celebrating this idea.
Mount Minakami is also home to one of Japan’s rare low-altitude breeding ponds for the {{ill|Japanese black salamander|en|Japanese black salamander|qid=Q2276273|lt=Japanese black salamander|1=Japanese black salamander}}<ref>[http://bunkazai-nagano.jp/modules/dbsearch/page1090.html "Japanese Black Salamander Breeding Pond at Mount Minakami"] - Nagano City Cultural Property Database (Accessed September 17, 2019)</ref>.
<gallery> File:Mount Minakami Lava Dome Relief Map, SRTM-1.jpg|Topographical map of the Nagano Basin and Mount Minakami (lower right) File:Minakami-Iwato.jpg|{{ill|Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|ja|岩戸神社 (長野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwato Shrine (Nagano City)|qq=}}, with a cave some claim is the entrance to the "pyramid" File:Minakami-jinjya 2.jpg|Torii gate of {{ill|Minakami Shrine|lt=Minakami Shrine|lt=Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja|熊野出速雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Izuhayao Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 皆神神社}} (Minakami Shrine) </gallery>
== In Fiction == * {{ill|Bio Booster Armor Guyver|en|Bio Booster Armor Guyver|qid=Q2632121|lt=Bio-Booster Armor Guyver|1=Bio Booster Armor Guyver}} – The mountain appears under the name "Minakami Mountain (魅奈神山)", located in the fictional "Takeyoricho, N City, N Prefecture". It is depicted as the ruins of an alien race (the Uranus), who experimented on Earth’s life forms, ultimately creating humanity as a weapon. * {{ill|Occult Academy|en|Occult Academy|qid=Q1523630|lt=Occult Academy|1=Occult Academy}} – The anime series sets the fictional "Occult Academy" at the summit of Mount Minakami.
